generations.text:
[
  {
    "card_title": "Gold Mining History at Mount Crichton Loop Track",
    "venue_name": "Mount Crichton Loop Track",
    "hook": "A loop walk that feels like a treasure hunt with tunnels and historic huts to explore.",
    "context": "This track is a local favorite for families because it combines physical activity with tangible history from the 1860s gold rush era.",
    "practical": "Located a 15-minute drive from Queenstown on the Glenorchy-Queenstown Road. The 8km loop takes about 2 hours to complete and is free to access.",
    "insight": "The 24-metre-long mining tunnel is safe to walk through; bring a small torch or use your phone light to make the experience extra exciting for the kids.",
    "consider": "The bridge at Māori Gully has had past washouts, so stick to the main marked path and check for mud if the forecast showers arrive on Saturday."
  },
  {
    "card_title": "The Iconic Queenstown Hill Time Walk",
    "venue_name": "Queenstown Hill Time Walk",
    "hook": "A steady climb rewarded by the 'Basket of Dreams' sculpture and a secret fairy forest.",
    "context": "This is the most popular walk in the area for a reason, offering the definitive panoramic view of Lake Wakatipu and the Remarkables.",
    "practical": "The trailhead is on Belfast Terrace, a 5-minute drive or 15-minute walk from the town center. It is a 5km return trip with a 500m elevation gain.",
    "insight": "Keep the kids motivated by looking for the 'fairy garden' about halfway up, where locals have tucked tiny houses and trinkets into the forest floor.",
    "consider": "It is a consistent uphill climb; if the children are very small, the lower lookout provides excellent views without needing to reach the true summit."
  },
  {
    "card_title": "Lakeside Reflections at Moke Lake Loop Track",
    "venue_name": "Moke Lake Loop Track",
    "hook": "An easy, flat circuit around a mirror-like lake tucked away in the mountains.",
    "context": "Locals head here to escape the downtown crowds. It is a peaceful spot where the mountains reflect perfectly on the water during calm mornings.",
    "practical": "Drive 15 minutes toward Glenorchy, then 5 minutes down the unsealed Moke Lake Road. The 6.5km loop is mostly flat and takes roughly 2 hours.",
    "insight": "This is a Department of Conservation (DOC) campground area, so it's a great spot to bring a picnic and let the kids toss rocks at the shore after the walk.",
    "consider": "The final stretch of the drive is on a gravel road; it is safe for standard cars, but you should take it slow to avoid dust and loose stones."
  }
]
